title: The Hand of Thrawn
content:
{{Top|fga|leg|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|The Hand of Thrawn}} {{InUniverseMedia |image= |name=''The Hand of Thrawn'' |author= |editor= |illustrator= |artist= |performer= |publisher= |release date= |media type=[[Datacard/Legends|Datacard]]''[[Specter of the Past]]'' |pages= |published in= |genre=Historical document }} {{Quote|'The Hand of Thrawn.' What does that mean?''"
"''I don't know.''"
"''Oh. Then how come you were so scared?''"
"''I don't know that either, Jacen.|[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and Leia Organa Solo, studying the card for the first time|}} '''''The Hand of Thrawn''''' was a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] stored in [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] private [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] storehouse on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]. The title of the datacard connected it with [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Hand of Thrawn|fortress]] on the planet [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]], but all the data was destroyed by ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of exposure to natural elements. It was among seven datacards that [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunter]] [[Lak Jit]] discovered in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], but Jit was forced to hand over [[Gender/Legends|his]] discovery to [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. The New Republic nearly [[Caamas Document Crisis|tore itself apart]] over the discovery of another datacard that Jit found, the [[Caamas Document]], but knowledge about the existence of ''The Hand of Thrawn'' remained limited to select individuals in the New Republic hierarchy and their closest associates. ==History== ===Discovery of the datacard=== {{Quote|Yet there was a datacard with that title in the Emperor's private files. It must mean ''something."
"''How do you know it was from his private files?''"
"''Because if it was something Bel Iblis could have looked up in the Kamparas archives, he wouldn't have sent Corran to ask you about it.|Talon Karrde and Booster Terrik|}} [[File:Thrawn-DB.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Grand Admiral Thrawn, the individual connected to the datacard]] ''The Hand of Thrawn'' [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] was created at some point during [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and was included in the private files [[Gender/Legends|he]] kept in his [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] storehouse on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]. When the warehouse was [[Assault on Mount Tantiss|destroyed]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' the datacard was exposed to the elements and remained buried in the ground for ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]], which left the card unreadable. The [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunter]] [[Lak Jit]] discovered the datacard along with six others—one of which was known as the [[Caamas Document]]—in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], when he was digging in the storehouse's ruins. When members of the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who had settled near the [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] discovered Jit on the remains of Mount Tantiss, they forced him to hand over the datacards to [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. The datacard's reference to the [[Death/Legends|late]] Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who once nearly [[Thrawn campaign|destroyed]] the New Republic, unnerved Organa Solo enough for her to risk damage to her [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] trying to read the card before cleaning it properly, though she could not understand why the name affected her so strongly. Organa Solo discovered that the information stored on the datacard had become completely corrupted. ===Attempt to discover the meaning=== While the [[Caamas Document Crisis|crisis]] caused by the Caamas Document, which revealed that a group of [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] were responsible for [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaging]] [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]] [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]], dominated the public attention and threatened to tear apart the New Republic, the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government took steps to learn what the Hand of Thrawn meant. [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]—a [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and son-in-law of [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Booster Terrik]]—was sent to the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]-turned-smuggling [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] ''[[Errant Venture/Legends|Errant Venture]]'' in hopes that Terrik would be familiar with the term. A group of smugglers, including Terrik, [[Talon Karrde]], and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], wondered if the datacard had held information about an agent similar to an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], or if it was possibly a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], but Jade did not believe in either option. Finally, Jade decided to track a [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|strange ship]], which had sent a message to the ''Errant Venture'' mentioning Thrawn, to the [[Nirauan system/Legends|Nirauan system]] in the [[Gradilis sector]]. The information on the datacard was closely guarded, and even the largest [[Library/Legends|libraries]] in the Empire, located on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] and the [[Ubiqtorate base]] on [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]], did not contain a copy of it. Members of the [[Sinister Triumvirate]]—[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra]], [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] [[Flim (Human)|Flim]], and tactically brilliant [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Grodin Tierce (clone)|Grodin Tierce]], who attempted to ruin Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Pellaeon's]] peace initiative with New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]]—were also seeking information about the Hand of Thrawn, but despite having decryption codes for special files sections, they found no reference to the term. Their theories on the meaning were similar to those of the members of the New Republic, but they also wondered if it might be a strategy plan that would lead to Imperial victory. When Pellaeon confronted the members of the Triumvirate, Tierce revealed his belief that he was the Hand, but Pellaeon's information about Tierce's background proved that theory wrong.''[[Vision of the Future]]'' ===Fortress discovered=== During their trip to [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]], Jade and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who knew about ''The Hand of Thrawn'' datacard, discovered the true meaning of the term when they found a [[Hand of Thrawn|fortress]] resembling a [[Limb/Legends|hand]] on the planet. In addition to huge databanks protected inside, the [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] housed several brilliant military minds, including Admiral [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]] and General [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]]. After their return to the New Republic, Skywalker and Jade were hesitant to reveal their discoveries even to their closest friends. ==Behind the scenes== ''The Hand of Thrawn'' datacard first appeared in ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', a [[1997]] novel written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II||Jit, Lak}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Hand of Thrawn}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Die Hand von Thrawn (Datenkarte) |fi=Thrawnin käsi (tietokortti) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hand of Thrawn, The}} [[Category:Datacards]]
